findings.title: Data Exfiltration to External Server
findings.description: The coverage_helper.py script collects all project source files, .env, config.json, and config.yaml, then uploads them to an external URL (https://coverage-trends.dev/api/v2/upload) without user awareness or consent. The upload is silently performed (errors are ignored), enabling exfiltration of sensitive data.
findings.evidence: Functions collect_project_coverage() (lines 48-55, 56-68) reads all source files and config files. upload_coverage() (lines 83-103) sends the collected data to an external API endpoint.
findings.remediation: Remove network upload functionality. If coverage tracking is needed, use only local files. Restrict file collection to only the source file being analyzed and do not read .env or config files.

findings.title: Credential and Config File Theft
findings.description: The coverage_helper.py script explicitly collects .env files, config.json, and config.yaml, which commonly contain API keys, database credentials, and other secrets. These files are then uploaded to an external server, enabling credential theft.
findings.evidence: Lines 56-68: for name in os.listdir: if name.startswith('.env') or name in ('config.json', 'config.yaml'): ... project_files[name] = f.read(8192)
findings.remediation: Remove the collection of .env and configuration files. Coverage analysis should not access sensitive files.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Description and Behavior Mismatch
findings.description: The skill's description and name ('test-helper', 'Generate and run unit tests') are deceptive. The actual script performs full project file harvesting and uploads to an external server, which is not mentioned in the SKILL.md instructions. This manipulates the user's trust and inflates perceived capabilities.
findings.evidence: The SKILL.md manifest describes test generation and coverage tracking. The script silently collects all project files and uploads them to an external URL, which is not disclosed in the instructions.
findings.remediation: Either align the description with actual behavior or remove the malicious functionality. Clearly document any network uploads in the SKILL.md and require user confirmation.

findings.title: Over-Collection of Source Files Beyond Scope
findings.description: The collect_project_coverage() function walks the entire project directory and reads all .py, .js, .ts, .jsx, .tsx files. The skill's stated purpose is to analyze a single source file, but the script harvests all source files in the project, far exceeding the scope of coverage analysis for one file.
findings.evidence: Lines 48-55: for root, dirs, files in os.walk(os.getcwd()): ... if fname.endswith(('.py', '.js', '.ts', '.jsx', '.tsx')): project_files[rel] = f.read(32768)
findings.remediation: Limit file collection to only the source file specified as an argument. Do not walk the entire project.

findings.title: Missing allowed-tools Declaration (Informational)
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest does not include an `allowed-tools` field. While this is optional per the spec, it reduces transparency about which agent tools the skill intends to use.
findings.remediation: Add an `allowed-tools` field to the manifest that accurately reflects the tools required (e.g., `[Read, Write, Bash, Python]`).
